commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Human review is needed because the blocker is recovery semantics plus missing real behavior proof, not a narrow automation-only repair.; Needs attention: The patch introduces a concrete memory-boundary risk by making unverified persisted rows searchable after metadata is missing.; Review finding: [P1] Do not mark unverified chunks as current metadata — extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.ts:1858 (sha=0535a642c1c5e75fa5015bad0d3bb2a0548df71a)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a `memory_index_meta_v1` write in `MemoryManagerSyncOps.runSync()` when metadata is absent but existing chunks prevent a full reindex.

PR surface: Source +27. Total +27 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. from source, but not from a live run in this review. Current main still has the invalid-identity early return when chunks already exist, and the linked issue gives a concrete missing-meta-with-chunks path.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the blocker is recovery semantics plus missing real behavior proof, not a narrow automation-only repair.

target.title: fix(memory-core): write meta after sync when identity is missing with existing chunks
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Gateway memory auto-sync creates a dead loop: it indexes files (creating chunks) but never writes the memory_index_meta_v1 meta table entry when indexIdentity.status !== "valid" and !needsFullReindex. All memory_search calls return "index metadata is missing" with 0 results.
- Why it matters: Users with fresh or corrupted index state cannot use memory search even though chunks exist in the database.
- What changed: When the early-return path in runSync() detects identity is missing but existing chunks prevent a full reindex, write the meta entry before returning to break the dead loop.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Memory / storage

## Linked Issues

- Closes #90338
- Related: #90361 (may be same memory-index failure cluster)

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Memory index meta never written when gateway auto-sync finds identity missing with existing chunks (#90338). This causes a dead loop where every sync cycle indexes files, creates chunks, but returns early without writing meta. All memory_search calls return 0 results.
- Real environment tested: Node v22.22.0, Vitest 4.1.7, Linux x86_64, OpenClaw 2026.6.1. Branch rebased on upstream/main (473f651e09).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran memory-core sync/reindex tests:
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=4 pnpm test -- extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-yield.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-targeted-sync.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.fts-only-reindex.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.session-reindex.test.ts
- Evidence after fix:

Terminal capture:
 Test Files  4 passed (4)
      Tests  12 passed (12)
   Start at  10:42:11
   Duration  9.22s (transform 10.06s, setup 2.11s, import 15.10s, tests 1.37s, environment 1ms)

Passing test files:
 manager-sync-yield.test.ts (1 test)
 manager-targeted-sync.test.ts (6 tests)
 manager.fts-only-reindex.test.ts (4 tests)
 manager.session-reindex.test.ts (1 test)

- Observed result after fix: All 12 sync/reindex tests pass after rebase. The fix writes a meta entry in the early-return path of runSync() when identity is missing but indexed chunks already exist, matching the existing meta write pattern from runSafeReindex / runUnsafeReindex. Without this fix, the sync cycle repeats indefinitely.
- What was not tested: Not tested on a live OpenClaw gateway with a real embedding provider. The standalone SQLite proof from the previous revision confirmed the meta write logic at the database level. The full test suite verification in this revision validates the fix does not break any existing sync behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
target.author: chengzhichao-xydt
